title: oVirt / RHV VDSM Host Reconnect Storm and Storage Lease Errors
app: Universal Forwarder on KVM hosts running VDSM, optional Engine-side aggregation
spl:
index=virtualization sourcetype="ovirt:vdsm" earliest=-2h
| search (lease OR "SAN lock" OR "storage domain" OR "Could not connect") AND (level="ERROR" OR level="WARN")
| stats count as events by host, storage_domain
| where events>=10
| sort -events
description: VDSM logs capture host-to-engine reconnect loops, SCSI reservation failures, and storage lease problems that make VMs pause or fence. These signatures often appear before the Engine UI shows hosts as non-operational.
value: Surfaces host-side storage and lease faults so network or SAN teams can act before mass VM pauses.
implementation: Deploy UF file inputs on each KVM host for `vdsm.log`. Use `props.conf` to extract `level` and key oVirt tokens. Create a baseline for normal reconnect chatter; alert when error-weighted volume spikes per host. Join to host inventory for rack awareness.
visualization: Table (hosts with lease errors), Timeline (VDSM errors), Choropleth by datacenter optional.

references.title: Red Hat — Collecting vdsm logs (RHV troubleshooting)

knownFalsePositives: Cluster events during planned hardware refresh, capacity adds, or scheduled maintenance modes.
